back_translation: Therefore, what shall we say? Shall we continue in sin, so that grace may multiply?
Absolutely not — we who have died to sin, how shall we still remain in it?
Or do you not know that all of us who received baptism into Jesus Christ also received baptism into His (royal) death?

Therefore, we were buried together with Him through baptism into death — so that, just as Christ (royally) was raised from the dead by the (royal) glory of the (royal) Father, so also we may live in newness of life
For if we have been bonded together with Him in the form of His (royal) death, we will also have a share in His (royal) resurrection
We know this — that our old self has been crucified together with Him, so that the body of sin may be rendered without power, so that we will no longer be slaves of sin
For the one who has died is released from sin
But if we have died together with Christ, we believe that we shall also live together with Him
We know that, when Christ (royally) was raised from the dead, He no longer (royally) dies — death has no more power over Him
For the (royal) death He died, He (royally) died to sin once for all — the life He (royally) lives, He (royally) lives for God
Therefore, you also — count yourselves dead to sin, but alive for God — in Christ Jesus
Therefore, do not let sin reign in your mortal body, so as to obey its lusts

And do not present the parts-of-your-body to sin as instruments of unrighteousness — but present yourselves to God as those whom He (royally) has brought to life from death; and present the parts-of-your-body to God as instruments of righteousness
For sin shall not have power over you any longer — because you are not under the Law, but under grace
So what then? Shall we sin, because we are not under the Law, but under grace? Absolutely not
Do you not know that the one to whom you present yourselves as slaves to obey, you are slaves to the one you obey — whether slaves of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness?
But thanks be to God that — though you used to be slaves of sin — you obeyed from the heart the form of teaching that (royally) was committed to you
When you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness

I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh — for just as you used to present the parts-of-your-body as slaves to impurity and lawlessness, leading to lawlessness that multiplies, so now you must present the parts-of-your-body as slaves to righteousness, leading to holiness
For when you used to be slaves of sin, you were free from righteousness
Therefore, in that time what fruit did you have from those things which now you are ashamed of? For the end-destination of those things is death
But now — when you have been set free from sin, and have become slaves of God — you have your fruit leading to holiness, and the end-destination is eternal life
For the wages of sin is death — but the gift of God is eternal life, in Christ Jesus our Lord
